description: Fresh W5 simulation with angle diversity + forbidden-phrase ban + judge-based scoring. Generated by evals/run-fresh-simulation.ts at 2026-04-26T05-16-54-081Z. judge_score values are NOT inside the strict EvalVariantSchema typed fields — they are surfaced via score (=judge_score/10).

variants.body: Did you know that Attio's API offers real-time data synchronization with no rate-limit roulette? Imagine integrating your CRM with internal systems, where every interaction is immediate and reliable. Our typed SDKs and webhook contracts mean fewer headaches and clearer communication, allowing engineers to focus on building instead of troubleshooting. With Attio, developers can experience an intuitive and flexible interface that truly supports innovation and efficiency, making integration a straightforward task rather than a complex challenge.
variants.score_reasoning: This text highlights Attio's API features and emphasizes ease of integration for developers.

variants.body: Imagine opening your CRM and finding exactly what you need in under a second—every time. With Attio's lightning-fast search feature, you can pinpoint contacts, notes, and updates without the frustration of lag or endless scrolling. It’s like having a well-organized desk where everything is just a keystroke away, allowing you to focus on what really matters: your clients. Every morning with Attio feels like stepping into a workspace where you have everything at your fingertips, making that first task of the day a pleasure, not a chore.
variants.score_reasoning: Highlights fast search and usability, emphasizing daily user experience.

variants.body: Have you ever faced the frustration of unexpected fees just when you're ready to scale? With Attio, what you see is what you get—transparent pricing with no hidden tiers and no surprises. Our straightforward approach ensures that every team member you add is covered under one simple plan, with no extra costs creeping in. Plus, our comprehensive feature set includes unlimited integrations to help you connect all your tools without any extra charges. So, as you grow, know that your CRM won't hold you back on pricing. It’s time to focus on what matters, without worrying about your budget.
variants.score_reasoning: This variant emphasizes clear and direct pricing structures, addressing common pain points with competitors, enhancing trust in Attio's model.

debug.judge_set_diversity_reasoning: The three variants tackle genuinely different angles: variant 0 focuses on search/UX speed for end-users, variant 1 addresses pricing transparency for budget-conscious buyers, and variant 2 targets developer experience with API/integration specifics. Each counter-narrative addresses a distinct competitor weakness (slow search, hidden fees, poor API). The audience and value proposition shift meaningfully across the set, demonstrating strong strategic diversity rather than superficial rewording.

judge_verdicts.judge_reasoning: This variant suffers from vague, aspirational language without concrete product details. Phrases like 'lightning-fast search,' 'under a second,' and 'well-organized desk' are generic claims lacking specificity—no actual performance metrics, no feature names beyond 'search.' The metaphor-heavy approach ('like stepping into a workspace') feels preachy and abstract rather than builder-confident. The tone is more inspirational than informative, missing the brand voice pillar of concrete numbers and product details over abstractions. No forbidden words detected, but the writing leans toward generic CRM marketing copy.

Multiple brand voice violations severely damage this variant. 'What you see is what you get' is cliché. The phrase 'focus on what matters' appears, which echoes forbidden territory. 'Without worrying about your budget' and 'won't hold you back' feel like settling-for-adjacent language. The copy uses vague claims ('transparent pricing,' 'straightforward approach,' 'comprehensive feature set') without concrete numbers—no actual price points, no specific tier details, no quantified integration limits. The rhetorical question opening and the preachy 'It's time to' construction feel condescending. Lacks the confident-builder voice entirely.

This is the strongest variant by far. It includes concrete product details: 'API,' 'real-time data synchronization,' 'typed SDKs,' 'webhook contracts'—actual technical features that demonstrate specificity. The phrase 'no rate-limit roulette' is creative and human-detectable. The target audience (engineers/developers) is clear, and the copy speaks to their pain points with technical precision. However, it still opens with a rhetorical question ('Did you know'), which feels slightly formulaic. The phrase 'focus on building' is acceptable but borders on generic. Overall, this reads like confident builder voice with real product substance, though it could push further on concrete numbers (e.g., actual rate limits, sync latency).
